report_name: Identity Attacks Now Top Ransomware Vector, Eclipsing Exploits

threat_actors.name: Ransomware Operators
threat_actors.description: A general class of threat actors who operate ransomware. According to the Sophos 'State of Ransomware 2026' report, these actors have shifted their primary initial access vector from exploiting vulnerabilities to compromising identities via phishing and stolen credentials.

attack_patterns.name: Phishing
attack_patterns.description: Used as the top initial access vector, where users are tricked into revealing credentials or executing a malicious attachment. Malicious emails and phishing together account for 50% of initial access events.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts
attack_patterns.description: After credentials are stolen, attackers use valid accounts to authenticate to systems and services, appearing as legitimate users to move through the network.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: The final stage of the attack, where victim data is encrypted to extort a ransom. This was successful in 56% of reported attacks.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol
attack_patterns.description: Data is exfiltrated as part of a double-extortion strategy before the final encryption stage.

attack_patterns.name: Remote Desktop Protocol
attack_patterns.description: Legitimate tools like RDP are used for lateral movement within the victim network after initial access is established.

attack_patterns.name: PowerShell
attack_patterns.description: PowerShell is used by attackers for discovery and lateral movement activities post-compromise.

attack_patterns.name: Kerberoasting
attack_patterns.description: Attackers may attempt to crack service account credentials by requesting Kerberos ticket-granting service (TGS) tickets, a technique known as Kerberoasting.

attack_patterns.name: System Owner/User Discovery
attack_patterns.description: Attackers use commands like 'whoami' to gather information about the current user context after gaining access.

tools.name: PowerShell
tools.description: Used by ransomware operators for discovery, lateral movement, and executing encoded commands within a compromised network.

tools.name: RDP
tools.description: Used by attackers for lateral movement across the victim's network using compromised credentials.

indicators.name: Kerberoasting Activity Detected
indicators.pattern:
title: Potential Kerberoasting Activity
id: 2b9a1f8e-7d3c-4b1a-9f8d-6e5c4b3a2f1e
status: experimental
description: Detects Kerberos TGS requests (Event ID 4769) with RC4-HMAC encryption, which is commonly associated with Kerberoasting attacks.
references:
  - https://www.sophos.com/en-us/blog/sophos-state-of-ransomware-2026
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-16
tags:
  - attack.t1558.003
  - attack.credential_access
level: medium
logsource:
  product: windows
  service: security
detection:
  selection:
    EventID: 4769
    TicketEncryptionType: '0x17' # RC4-HMAC
    ServiceName|contains:
      - 'mssql'
      - 'http'
      - 'cifs'
  filter_machine:
    ServiceName|endswith: '$'
  condition: selection and not filter_machine
indicators.description: Detects Kerberos TGS requests (Kerberoasting) which can indicate attempts to crack service account credentials, as mentioned in the report's hunting hints.

indicators.name: PowerShell Encoded Command Execution
indicators.pattern:
title: PowerShell Execution with Encoded Command
id: 8c4d5e6f-1a2b-4c3d-8e9f-0a1b2c3d4e5f
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of PowerShell with an encoded command argument, a common technique used by attackers to obfuscate malicious scripts.
references:
  - https://www.sophos.com/en-us/blog/sophos-state-of-ransomware-2026
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-16
tags:
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.execution
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: 
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
    CommandLine|contains:
      - ' -e '
      - ' -en '
      - ' -enc '
      - ' -enco'
      - ' -encod'
      - ' -encode'
      - ' -encoded'
      - ' -encodedc'
      - ' -encodedco'
      - ' -encodedcom'
      - ' -encodedcomm'
      - ' -encodedcomma'
      - ' -encodedcomman'
      - ' -encodedcommand'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects PowerShell commands with encoded payloads, a common technique for obfuscating malicious activity mentioned in the report's hunting hints.

indicators.name: Execution of 'whoami' Discovery Command
indicators.pattern:
title: Whoami Execution For Discovery
id: f1a2b3c4-d5e6-4f7a-8b9c-0d1e2f3a4b5c
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of 'whoami' with the '/all' flag, which is often used by attackers to gather detailed user and group information after gaining initial access.
references:
  - https://www.sophos.com/en-us/blog/sophos-state-of-ransomware-2026
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-16
tags:
  - attack.t1033
  - attack.discovery
level: low
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\whoami.exe'
    CommandLine|contains: '/all'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution of 'whoami /all', a common discovery command used by attackers after gaining initial access to gather user and group information.
